key_insight: The paper derives a predictive law that decomposes LLM ensemble lift into rescue and damage masses, showing that diversity of thought can be quantified and used to forecast ensemble gains.
engineering_relevance: This gives practitioners a practical way to estimate when weighted model ensembles will outperform individual LLMs, helping choose vote splits and model combinations before deployment.
method: Derive an exact ensemble-lift decomposition into rescue and damage mass.
Define predictive metrics: accuracy-adjusted correctness correlation (φ_adj), accuracy gap, and collective accuracy.
Calibrate a heuristic on one benchmark, then test transfer with frozen coefficients on unseen datasets.
evidence_signal: Evaluated on 767,520 inferences from ten open-weight models across two graduate science benchmarks plus a forensic multi-turn tool-use benchmark; the heuristic achieved Spearman ρ=0.84 on calibration, ρ=0.51 and 0.84 on transfer tasks, and swap mass tracked realized lift with R²≥0.96.
watch_item: Results depend on calibration at a specific 40:60 vote split, so generalization to other ensemble sizes, domains, or routing policies may require re-calibration.
paper_title: Quantifying Diversity of Thought: A Predictive Law of Weighted LLM Ensemble Lift
